campaign: Clear Creek Mountain Community Press Kit

angle.coreStory: Henderson Mine is not just a mine story. It is a Colorado mountain community story about jobs, molybdenum, water, safety, long-term operations, local partnerships, and what comes next for Clear Creek and Grand counties.
angle.workingHeadline: Inside Henderson Mine: The Colorado Molybdenum Operation Connecting Work, Water, and Mountain Communities
angle.nutGraf: Henderson Mine, operated by Climax Molybdenum Company, is an underground molybdenum operation near Empire, Colorado, with its mill across the Continental Divide in Grand County.

quickFacts: Henderson Mine is operated by Climax Molybdenum Company, a Freeport-McMoRan company.
The mine is near Empire, Georgetown, and Idaho Springs; the Henderson mill site is in Grand County.
The mine and mill are separated by the Continental Divide and connected by a 10-mile conveyor tunnel plus an additional surface conveyor.
Public materials describe the operation as a large block-cave underground mining complex feeding a concentrator.
Q1 2026 community materials listed approximately 425 employees and 80 contractors.
Public materials say the Northwest panel supports the current life-of-mine plan through 2038.

contacts.corporateHeadquarters: 4340 E. Cotton Center Blvd., Suite 110, Phoenix, AZ 85040
contacts.hendersonListing: Climax Molybdenum Company, Henderson Mine, P.O. Box 68, Empire, CO 80438; +1 303.569.3221

interviewQuestions: What role does Henderson Mine play in Climax Molybdenum's Colorado strategy today?
What should local readers understand about molybdenum that they probably do not know?
What does the current life-of-mine plan through 2038 mean for employees and nearby communities?
Walk us through a normal day at Henderson without using technical jargon.
What part of the operation would surprise a first-time visitor?
What is the first safety lesson every new worker learns?
What are the main water systems Henderson manages?
How does the Community Partnership Panel work?
What local needs are most pressing right now?
When people drive past this area, what do you hope they understand about the people working here?

mustGet: Henderson Mine sign / entrance area, only where approved.
Wide mountain landscape showing the high-country setting.
Workers in PPE walking, talking, checking equipment, or entering approved work areas.
Portraits of leadership and workers with mountains, equipment, or site buildings behind them.
Community visuals: schools, Main Street, trail signs, parks, storefronts.
avoidUnlessApproved: License plates, badges, personal contact info, or sensitive operational boards.
Any underground footage unless the company explicitly approves and escorts it.

mustVerify: Correct spelling and title of every person interviewed.
Whether comments are on the record, background, or off the record.
Current site manager / general manager name and title.
Whether life-of-mine through 2038 is still the preferred public phrasing.
Exact credit line for the attached partner artwork.
Whether the company wants Henderson Mine, Henderson operations, or Henderson Mine and Mill.
Safety restrictions for photo, video, drone, and underground access.
Any required technical-accuracy review before publication.
guardrails: Do not publish unverified titles, headcount, production, or life-of-mine language as final.
Do not shoot restricted areas, security infrastructure, screens, badges, or underground footage without explicit approval.
Treat recent public staff names as references that require on-site title verification.
Use the live Register-Call story as the public proof path and this SATCOM page as the internal field kit.
